arguments.query: Google OAuth2 Python Flask authorization code flow

turns.content:
1. Google OAuth2 for Web Server Apps - https://developers.google.com/identity/protocols/oauth2/web-server
   Authorization code flow for server-side apps. Redirect user, exchange code for tokens.
2. Authlib - Python OAuth library - https://authlib.org/
   pip install authlib. Supports OAuth 1/2, OpenID Connect.
3. Flask-Dance Google OAuth - https://flask-dance.readthedocs.io/en/latest/providers.html
   Flask extension for OAuth with Google and other providers.

Using OAuth 2.0 for Web Server Applications

1. Create OAuth credentials in Google Cloud Console
2. Redirect user to:
   https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth?client_id=...&redirect_uri=...&response_type=code&scope=openid email profile
3. User authorizes, Google redirects to your redirect_uri with ?code=...
4. Exchange code for tokens:
   POST https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token
   Body: code, client_id, client_secret, redirect_uri, grant_type=authorization_code
5. Use access_token to get user info:
   GET https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v2/userinfo
   Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
   Returns: id, email, name, picture

arguments.title: Google OAuth2 authorization code flow
arguments.content:
1. Redirect to https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth with client_id, redirect_uri, response_type=code, scope=openid email profile
2. Exchange code at POST https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token with code, client_id, client_secret, redirect_uri, grant_type=authorization_code
3. Get user info at GET https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v2/userinfo with Bearer token
Returns: id, email, name, picture

arguments.summary: Researched Google OAuth2 flow. Will implement authorize redirect and callback handler using requests.

next_steps.description: Add Google OAuth config vars to src/config.py
Create src/auth/google_oauth.py with authorize and callback functions

turns.content: Added content after line 4 in src/config.py

arguments.content:
import urllib.parse
import requests
from flask import redirect, request, session, url_for, Blueprint
from config import GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET
from models.user import User
from database import db_session

google_bp = Blueprint('google_auth', __name__)

AUTH_URL = 'https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth'
TOKEN_URL = 'https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token'
USERINFO_URL = 'https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v2/userinfo'


@google_bp.route('/auth/google')
def authorize():
    callback = request.host_url.rstrip('/') + '/auth/google/callback'
    params = urllib.parse.urlencode({
        'client_id': GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID,
        'redirect_uri': callback,
        'response_type': 'code',
        'scope': 'openid email profile',
    })
    return redirect(f'{AUTH_URL}?{params}')


@google_bp.route('/auth/google/callback')
def callback():
    code = request.args.get('code')
    if not code:
        return 'Authorization failed', 400
    callback_url = request.host_url.rstrip('/') + '/auth/google/callback'
    token_resp = requests.post(TOKEN_URL, data={
        'code': code,
        'client_id': GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID,
        'client_secret': GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET,
        'redirect_uri': callback_url,
        'grant_type': 'authorization_code',
    }, timeout=10)
    token_resp.raise_for_status()
    access_token = token_resp.json()['access_token']
    user_resp = requests.get(USERINFO_URL, headers={
        'Authorization': f'Bearer {access_token}'
    }, timeout=10)
    user_resp.raise_for_status()
    info = user_resp.json()
    user = User.query.filter_by(email=info['email']).first()
    if not user:
        user = User(email=info['email'], name=info['name'])
        db_session.add(user)
        db_session.commit()
    session['user_id'] = user.id
    return redirect('/')

arguments.message: Google OAuth2 login is set up. The flow: GET /auth/google redirects to Google, then Google calls back to /auth/google/callback where we exchange the code, get user info, create or find the user, and set the session. Set GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET env vars, and add the callback URL to your Google Cloud Console OAuth config.

arguments.summary: Implemented Google OAuth2 login with authorization code flow. Created google_oauth.py with authorize redirect and callback handler. Auto-creates users on first login.
arguments.final_answer: Google OAuth2 login implemented. GET /auth/google starts the flow, /auth/google/callback handles the return. Users are auto-created on first login. Set GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET env vars.
